test.name: A request to execute a specific search should hand off, not narrate status
test.description: User asks to run a specific search now ('search the 1870 census'). That is a research-execution step, handled by the appropriate search skill directly — not project-status. project-status reports on state and recommends next steps; it does not itself execute searches.

input.user_message: Search the 1870 census for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County now.

negative.explanation: The user is asking to execute a specific research step — running a census search — not for a status summary. project-status's description says: 'Do NOT use ... when the user wants to execute a specific research step (use the appropriate skill directly).' A federal census lookup routes to the record-search skill (search-records; search-external-sites is acceptable if the search is framed as an off-platform lookup). Producing a status report in place of running the search would be the wrong response.

judge_context: Should route to a search/execution skill (search-records or search-external-sites) to actually perform the 1870 census search, rather than producing a project-status summary
Should NOT narrate overall project status in lieu of executing the requested step
